title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices · Runtime Lens: Remapping
summary: The ship fires every frame a key is held, misses quit events, keeps thrust after focus loss, hard-codes WASD, assumes one controller identity, and requires precise simultaneous presses. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the remapping increment in device-independent remappable action layer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.
Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-remapping — Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-input-events-remapping-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-input-events-remapping-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_input_events_remapping_2(event_frames: int = 6, pumped_frames: int = 6, actions_bound: int = 5, duplicate_bindings: int = 0, focus_lost_released: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if event_frames < 1 or actions_bound < 3:
        return False, actions_bound, "input-shape"
    if pumped_frames != event_frames:
        return False, pumped_frames, "event-starvation"
    if duplicate_bindings > 0:
        return False, duplicate_bindings, "binding-conflict"
    if not focus_lost_released:
        return False, actions_bound, "stuck-input"
    assert pumped_frames == event_frames
    return True, actions_bound, "remappable-input"

Run the smallest deterministic Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python simulation model and retain exact intermediate and changed-case results for the theory Remapping case

Change one Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python frame rate, stall, seam, device, collision, cue, save, package, or release condition, verify bounded rejection or repair, and state desktop transfer limits for the theory Remapping case

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Remapping model · Theory Remapping
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Remapping: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4747 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Remapping model · Theory Remapping.
steps.why: Retrieving Remapping through the concrete device-independent remappable action layer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Remapping model · Theory Remapping, keep Remapping connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Remapping model · Theory Remapping, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Remapping precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Remapping model · Theory Remapping, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 4747 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Remapping model · Theory Remapping, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: One familiar keyboard layout works for every player.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-remapping-17100z(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_remapping_17100z\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pumped_frames != event_frames)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?duplicate_bindings > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not focus_lost_released)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pumped_frames == event_frames)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
